us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran With Deadline, Threats to Infrastructure
us-politics.body:
Trump set a Tuesday night deadline for Iran to accept a nuclear deal, warning that the country 'could be taken out in one night' and threatening strikes on civilian infrastructure if no agreement is reached. He later extended the deadline, a move former National Security Advisor John Bolton says signals to Tehran that Trump 'isn't really serious.' Iran has rejected a ceasefire proposal, with a former NATO commander noting the regime still holds leverage.

Separately, Trump detailed a rescue operation that recovered a downed F-15 pilot from Iran, framing it as a military success. Defense Secretary Hegseth drew attention by linking the rescue to the Easter story. Trump also threatened to pursue whoever leaked details of the mission. Senator Bernie Sanders called Trump's threats the 'ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual.' Critics on the right and left remain divided on whether the pressure campaign is working.

sources.title: Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
Former NATO commander on Iran rejecting ceasefire proposal: ‘They still have cards to play’

Bolton: Trump extending deadline shows Iran ‘he’s not really serious’
Hegseth ties Iran rescue to Easter story and Jesus Christ: 'A pilot reborn'
Trump says US will go after ‘leaker’ of info on downed pilot in Iran
Sanders on Trump Iran f-bomb threat: ‘Ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual’

us-politics.headline: Trump Clashes With Supreme Court, Pushes Hormuz Toll Idea
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over rulings on birthright citizenship and tariffs, escalating his friction with the judiciary. The criticism follows a pattern of the administration testing legal boundaries on immigration and trade policy.

On a separate front, Trump floated charging foreign ships a toll to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a major global oil chokepoint. OPEC+ has agreed to boost output once the strait reopens, signaling markets are watching the Iran standoff closely. The toll proposal drew skepticism from analysts who question US legal authority over an international waterway.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage

us-politics.headline: GOP Eyes Senate Majority With $342M War Chest
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment targeting 2026 Senate races, signaling an early and aggressive push to hold and expand the GOP majority. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor, with Alme pledging to keep the seat red in what is expected to be a competitive race.

In Arizona, Republicans forced a legislative probe into a county's policies limiting cooperation with ICE, putting the Democratic attorney general under pressure to respond. The Education Department also rescinded Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity policies, a move supporters call a return to biological definitions and critics say strips protections from transgender students.

sources.title: Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity

us-politics.headline: Bannon Conviction Dismissed, AP Cuts Dozens of Staff
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon, clearing the way to dismiss his January 6 contempt conviction. The ruling is a significant legal win for Bannon and adds to ongoing debate about accountability for those involved in the Capitol riot.

In media, the Associated Press announced it is cutting dozens of staffers as part of a restructuring. CBS confirmed it will replace Stephen Colbert's Late Show time slot with Byron Allen's Comics Unleashed. The AP cuts come as several major news organizations continue to reduce headcount amid financial pressure.

sources.title: Associated Press cutting dozens of staffers
Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
CBS announces what’s taking over Colbert’s ‘Late Show’ time slot

au-politics.headline: Trump slams Australia as Iran rejects ceasefire deal
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump declared Iran could be "taken out tomorrow night" and accused Australia of failing to help in the Middle East conflict, marking a fresh diplomatic friction between Washington and Canberra. Iran has rejected a truce deal, leaving the war's trajectory uncertain. The remarks follow earlier Trump criticism of Australia's role in the region.

The conflict is hitting Australian households directly. PVC prices are surging as Middle East supply chains fracture, squeezing the building and construction sector. Fuel costs are also climbing, though energy experts warn that expanding domestic oil drilling would not resolve the underlying crisis. Markets are watching closely, with the ASX tracking Wall Street gains as oil trades around US$109 a barrel.

sources.title: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; ‘increasing’ cyclone threat for Queensland
Middle East war causes PVC prices to skyrocket
Live: ASX poised for higher open as Wall St rises
Live: Energy expert says more drilling for oil won't fix fuel crisis
Trump threatens 'demolition' of Iran and takes another swipe at Australia
Morning Mail: ‘scam’ empire links alleged in Timor-Leste, Trump says Iran could be ‘taken out tomorrow night’
Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Iran rejects truce deal

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila threatens Far North Queensland coast
au-politics.body:
Tropical Cyclone Maila is forecast to cross Far North Queensland, with authorities warning the threat is increasing. Communities in the region are preparing for potential flooding and destructive winds. The Bureau of Meteorology is monitoring the system closely as it tracks toward the coast.

The cyclone adds pressure to a Queensland environment already under stress. Separately, wrecked boats in the Great Barrier Reef have been leaking styrofoam and chemicals for months, with salvage timelines drawing sharp criticism from conservationists and local operators. The slow response raises questions about federal and state responsibility for marine pollution in one of the world's most protected ecosystems.

sources.title: Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months
News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; ‘increasing’ cyclone threat for Queensland
Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

au-politics.headline: Aged care failures and hospital errors expose system cracks
au-politics.body:
A Canberra woman with paraplegia has spent 19 months stranded in unsuitable accommodation, unable to access an aged care bed that meets her needs. Her case highlights what advocates describe as a structural gap between disability and aged care systems, leaving vulnerable people without a clear pathway to support.

In a separate incident, a Western Australian hospital has apologised after a man requiring urgent surgery was sent home, later developing compartment syndrome - a serious and potentially limb-threatening condition. Both cases are drawing renewed scrutiny of resourcing and triage decisions across Australia's public health and aged care systems.

sources.title: Hospital apologises after man who needed urgent surgery was sent home
Mary has fallen into an aged care 'black hole' and sees no way out

au-politics.headline: Election watch: Liberals eye Victoria, independents gain ground
au-politics.body:
The Liberal Party faces a strategic question about whether it can rebuild in Victoria after successive state and federal losses, with internal debate continuing over whether the party's positioning on culture and policy is alienating urban voters. The Spectator argues structural and demographic shifts make recovery difficult but not impossible.

Meanwhile, a pro-climate independent candidate in the regional seat of Farrer has received backing from Rupert Murdoch's son-in-law, a notable split from the media empire's traditional conservative alignment. In South Australia, First Nations voters reported being questioned about their identity at polling booths during the state's Voice to Parliament vote, prompting calls for a formal review of the experience.

sources.title: Can the Liberals win back Victoria?
'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths
Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

world.headline: US-Israel Strike Iran as Hormuz Deadline Looms
world.body:
American and Israeli forces have struck targets across Iran including the Bushehr nuclear power plant, a top university, and residential areas, with rescue teams still pulling survivors from rubble. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed strikes are intensifying. Trump warned his deadline for Iran to surrender control of the Strait of Hormuz is final, and floated charging foreign ships for passage through the waterway.

Iran has pushed a proposal to end the war while Hezbollah and the Houthis launched coordinated strikes on Israel in solidarity with Tehran. North Korea, however, is keeping its distance. Trump separately claimed Iranians are begging the US to continue bombing - a remark drawing sharp criticism from Democrats, who accused him of threatening war crimes. Republicans largely backed the campaign.

sources.title: Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
Why is Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant being attacked?
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
Trump warns Hormuz deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war

world.headline: US Airman Rescued from Iran; Reporter Threatened Over Leak
world.body:
A US airman was recovered from a remote area of Iran in a covert rescue operation, details of which the BBC has outlined. The mission's success was short-lived as a political flashpoint - Trump publicly threatened to jail a reporter who disclosed details of the operation before the White House was ready to announce it.

The incident adds to mounting tensions over press freedom as the administration wages an active military campaign against Iran.

sources.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak

world.headline: Gaza and Lebanon Bloodshed Deepens as Protests Erupt in Tel Aviv
world.body:
An Israeli airstrike killed at least ten Palestinians near a school in Gaza, while clashes between Hamas and a militia added to the death toll. Israeli forces simultaneously deepened a ground offensive in Lebanon, with fresh airstrikes pounding the country. Soldiers fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron.

The widening violence drew protesters to the US embassy in Tel Aviv, where demonstrators demanded an end to the wars. The rare demonstration inside Israel reflects growing domestic unease alongside the international outcry.

sources.title: Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars

world.headline: India Death Sentences, Russia Jails Governor, Artemis Record
world.body:
An Indian court sentenced nine police officers to death for killing detainees during Covid-era custody, a rare conviction in a case that exposed systemic abuse of lockdown powers. In Russia, the former governor of Kursk was jailed in a corruption probe linked to Ukraine's cross-border incursion into the region last year.

On a brighter note, NASA's Artemis II crew broke the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth, marking a milestone in the push to return astronauts to the Moon.

sources.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth

ai.headline: OpenAI Leadership Crisis Deepens as Alums Quietly Back Rivals
ai.body:
Trust in Sam Altman is fracturing inside OpenAI, with insiders describing him as the core problem in the company's ongoing governance tensions. The concerns echo the 2023 board revolt and show the unease has not faded.

Meanwhile, former OpenAI employees have been quietly deploying a fund that could reach $100 million, backing AI startups outside the company's orbit. That capital flight signals that some of the people who built OpenAI are now betting against its monopoly on the future.

OpenAI is also pushing a broader economic vision - public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek - framing AI disruption as manageable with the right policy. Critics will note the company is simultaneously the source of that disruption.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek

ai.headline: Iran Threatens Stargate Data Centers as AI Infrastructure Becomes a Target
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers, specifically targeting the Abu Dhabi facility under development in the UAE. The threat marks a sharp escalation - critical AI infrastructure is now explicitly in the crosshairs of state-level adversaries.

Stargate, the $500 billion AI infrastructure initiative backed by OpenAI, SoftBank, and Oracle, has moved fast to build capacity in the Gulf. Placing major nodes in geopolitically exposed regions now looks like a liability. The threats underscore a tension that has received little attention: the physical vulnerability of the data centers powering the AI boom.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: Google Launches Offline AI Dictation While AI Singer Floods iTunes
ai.body:
Google quietly released an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, processing speech entirely on-device without sending audio to the cloud. It is a notable privacy-forward move from a company that built its business on data collection.

On the stranger end of AI's cultural footprint, a fake AI singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart. No human, no real artist - just generated tracks flooding a chart that once meant something. Together, the two stories capture AI's expanding reach: useful and mundane in one corner, disruptive and destabilising in another.

sources.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart

ai.headline: macOS Has a TCP Time Bomb and a Safari Recovery Mode Exploit
ai.body:
Researchers found that macOS stops TCP networking entirely after 49.7 days of uptime - a classic integer overflow tied to a millisecond counter wrapping around. Machines that stay on long enough simply lose network access until rebooted. It is the kind of bug that hides in production for years.

Separately, a security researcher demonstrated root persistence through macOS Recovery Mode via Safari, achieving unrestricted write access to the filesystem. Recovery Mode is supposed to be a trusted environment. The two findings together paint a picture of compounding reliability and security gaps in Apple's desktop platform.

sources.title: A macOS bug that causes TCP networking to stop working after 49.7 days
Root Persistence via macOS Recovery Mode Safari

btc.headline: Bitcoin Climbs Past $70K as Strategy Hoovers Up Supply
btc.body:
Bitcoin pushed back above $70,000 Monday, driven partly by reports of Iran ceasefire talks and contrarian signals that a local bottom may be forming. Profit-taking has capped the rally, keeping price volatile in a tight range.

Strategy added another 4,871 BTC for $330 million, bringing its total holdings to nearly 767,000 BTC - absorbing roughly three times the new supply mined since its last purchase. The firm is sitting on paper losses exceeding $14.5 billion from Q1, yet keeps buying. Analysts now see a path to $110,000 if institutional demand continues at this pace.

The options market tells a more cautious story. Traders are quietly pricing in significant downside protection, suggesting the rally has skeptics even as on-chain bottoming signals accumulate.

sources.title: Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains

btc.headline: Dimon Warns of Blockchain Rivals as JPMorgan Races to Adapt
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual shareholder letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as genuine competitive threats, naming them alongside fintech and AI as forces reshaping finance. The bank runs its own blockchain unit, Kinexys, but Dimon signaled the pace of change demands faster internal action.

The comments land as tokenization moves from experiment to infrastructure. Bernstein analysts separately argued that Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could double its stock price, pointing to loan growth as the key driver. Bitmine, meanwhile, moved its NYSE listing forward as its ether treasury hit 4.8 million ETH.

Institutional crypto is increasingly running through prime brokerages, with custody, lending, and execution converging into single relationships - a structure that mirrors traditional finance more closely than crypto's origins suggest.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE
The future of institutional crypto runs through prime brokerages

btc.headline: Polymarket Overhauls Exchange, Kalshi Wins Court Battle
btc.body:
Polymarket launched a full exchange upgrade, replacing USDC.e with a native USDC-backed token and taking direct control of its trading and resolution infrastructure. The move reduces reliance on third-party systems and positions the platform to compete more aggressively as prediction markets gain mainstream traction.

Kalshi scored a parallel legal win. A US appeals court upheld a block on New Jersey's attempt to shut down its sports prediction markets, reinforcing federal CFTC jurisdiction over state-level enforcement. The ruling is a significant precedent for the sector.

On the regulatory calendar, a Senate Banking panel member confirmed an April timeline for crypto market structure legislation, adding pressure on Congress to act before the window closes.

sources.title: Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi
US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

btc.headline: Aave Loses Risk Manager as DeFi Governance Strains Show
btc.body:
Chaos Labs, one of Aave's primary risk managers, is departing the protocol amid a broader contributor exodus and unresolved governance disputes. The split highlights a recurring tension in large DeFi protocols - paid contributors and token holders often disagree on strategy, compensation, and accountability, with no clean mechanism to resolve it.

Onchain perpetual DEX volumes have fallen for five straight months since an October peak, with Hyperliquid holding its share while smaller venues bleed. The data suggests the DeFi trading boom has cooled significantly.

A new AI-powered cybercrime tool is also circulating, targeting crypto and banking KYC systems through deepfakes. The tool lowers the barrier for identity fraud at a moment when institutions are expanding onchain activity.

sources.title: Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes
New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
Onchain perp DEX volumes fall for five straight months after October peak

food.name: Miso-Glazed Aubergine (Nasu Dengaku)
food.description: Halved aubergines broiled until caramelised under a sweet-savoury white miso and mirin glaze
food.tip: Score the flesh in a crosshatch before glazing so the miso penetrates deeply rather than just sitting on the surface

exercise.name: Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy shopping bags, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer carries, suitcase carries (one side only), and overhead carries in 40-metre lengths to build grip strength, core stability, and posture after a weekend of likely sitting

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity Over Time
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers like Derek Parfit extended this brutally into human identity, arguing that the 'you' of ten years ago may be no more continuous with today's you than a stranger
learn.why: On a Monday at the start of a new week it is a quietly destabilising and useful hour — Parfit's conclusions in Reasons and Persons suggest that loosening your grip on a fixed self actually reduces anxiety and increases ethical concern for others
